message:
feat(rchd): add periodic triage loop and on-demand triage command (bd-vvmd.5.4)

Extends process_triage module with:
- TriageLoop: configurable background sweep with workload-aware throttling
- TriageCommand: on-demand triage sharing the same RemediationPipeline
- Budget-aware sweeps with partial-result semantics on exhaustion
- Worker filtering for scoped on-demand runs
- EventBus sweep completion events for observability
- 12 new tests covering cadence, filtering, budget, determinism

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -440,7 +440,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","title":"Define process_triage integration contract and safe-action policy","description":"Specify how rchd invokes process_triage and interprets findings. Define strict allowlist/denylist and escalation ladder to avoid terminating legitimate workloads. Include rollback and observability requirements.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Contract specifies request/response schema, timeout/retry policy, and supported triage action classes.
- Safe-action policy defines explicit allowlist, denylist, and escalation thresholds.
- Contract includes audit requirements for every invoked triage/remediation action.
- Failure taxonomy distinguishes detector uncertainty from executor/runtime failures.
- Test plan covers contract conformance and policy enforcement edge cases.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:06.959266657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T19:33:02.351966388Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T19:33:02.351940199Z","close_reason":"Completed: contract/schema coverage and validation gates passing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contract","process_triage","rch-rel-2026q1","safety"],"comments":[{"id":216,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Paused to execute current bv --robot-next top pick bd-vvmd.3.1; partial contract draft kept locally for later continuation.","created_at":"2026-02-16T21:05:55Z"},{"id":223,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: process_triage contract code in rch-common was previously not in the crate module graph (rch_common::e2e::mod did not declare/re-export process_triage), so contract/tests were effectively dormant. Wired module into rch-common e2e public surface ( + explicit re-exports), then fixed schema test resilience for schemars layout variants and resolved a clippy  finding in allow/deny conflict detection. Validation: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --workspace --all-targets; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p rch-common process_triage_contract_ -- --nocapture.","created_at":"2026-02-17T02:02:46Z"},{"id":224,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Correction to previous comment (shell quoting artifact removed): process_triage contract code in rch-common was previously outside the active crate module graph because rch-common/src/e2e/mod.rs did not declare or re-export the process_triage module. Wired it into the public e2e surface via module declaration and explicit re-exports, then fixed schema-test robustness for schemars definition layouts and replaced the allow/deny overlap check with a clippy-compliant first-intersection lookup. Validation: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --workspace --all-targets; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p rch-common process_triage_contract_ -- --nocapture.","created_at":"2026-02-17T02:02:53Z"},{"id":226,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Validation pass (2026-02-17): process_triage contract now includes explicit response schema coverage for required audit fields and failure-kind taxonomy; targeted and workspace checks all pass via rch offload. Commands: rch exec -- cargo fmt --check; rch exec -- cargo test -p rch-common process_triage_contract_ -- --nocapture; rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets; rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings.","created_at":"2026-02-17T19:32:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.5.2","title":"Implement stuck-process detector correlated with build lifecycle state","description":"Detect candidate stuck/orphan processes by correlating remote process state, timeouts, slot ownership, and build metadata. Goal: high precision detection with minimal false positives.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Detector correlates process state, build ownership, slot allocation, and heartbeat/progress evidence.
- Confidence scoring differentiates slow-but-healthy jobs from likely orphan/hung processes.
- Detection output includes explainable evidence fields for operator review.
- Detector latency stays within configured triage budget under concurrent workloads.
- Unit/integration tests cover jitter, transient telemetry gaps, and false-positive suppression.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:07.166793804Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T20:31:29.721435734Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T20:31:29.721417169Z","close_reason":"Completed: multi-signal stuck detector now correlates process liveness, slot ownership, build age, and heartbeat/progress evidence with operator-visible detector snapshots","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["daemon","detection","process_triage","rch-rel-2026q1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:16.608936776Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:15.940719864Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":151,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Detection quality bar: never classify by runtime alone. Correlate PID trees with build ownership, slot lifecycle, command fingerprints, and timeout envelopes. Model should support confidence levels so remediation policy can require higher confidence for destructive actions.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:28:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","title":"Implement bounded remediation pipeline (TERM/KILL escalation with auditing)","description":"Add remediation executor with conservative escalation, bounded retries, and full audit logging. Must never apply broad destructive process actions and must preserve system safety invariants.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Remediation pipeline enforces bounded escalation (observe -> TERM -> KILL) with explicit guardrails.
- Actions are scoped to verified offender processes only; no broad kill patterns are permitted.
- Every action emits audit record with evidence, rationale, actor, and outcome.
- Pipeline supports abort/rollback behavior when confidence drops or safety checks fail.
- Integration tests validate escalation correctness, safety boundaries, and audit completeness.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:07.370040536Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T07:44:00.274842057Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T07:44:00.274822962Z","close_reason":"Implemented bounded remediation pipeline with TERM/KILL escalation and auditing. 20 tests, 0 clippy warnings.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["process_triage","rch-rel-2026q1","remediation","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:07:29.723547694Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:16.809138818Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":191,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Dependency tightened: remediation executor now explicitly depends on integration safety contract (bd-vvmd.5.1) to prevent implementation drift between policy and action pipeline.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:14Z"},{"id":230,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claimed by RainyHorse. Starting bounded remediation pipeline implementation.","created_at":"2026-02-18T07:16:17Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-vvmd.5.4","title":"Add periodic triage loop + on-demand operator triage command","description":"Provide scheduled triage and manual trigger paths, with configurable cadence and time budgets. Expose triage outcomes in machine-readable status output and human remediation hints.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Periodic loop cadence is configurable and does not starve active scheduling paths.
- On-demand command supports scoped triage runs with clear machine/human output modes.
- Loop and command share deterministic decision pipeline to avoid divergent behavior.
- Time-budget exhaustion is surfaced explicitly with partial-result semantics.
- Tests verify scheduling cadence, manual trigger behavior, and output determinism.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:07.576921402Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:45:44.189818174Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["maintenance","operations","process_triage","rch-rel-2026q1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:17.006389954Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-vvmd.5.4","title":"Add periodic triage loop + on-demand operator triage command","description":"Provide scheduled triage and manual trigger paths, with configurable cadence and time budgets. Expose triage outcomes in machine-readable status output and human remediation hints.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Periodic loop cadence is configurable and does not starve active scheduling paths.
- On-demand command supports scoped triage runs with clear machine/human output modes.
- Loop and command share deterministic decision pipeline to avoid divergent behavior.
- Time-budget exhaustion is surfaced explicitly with partial-result semantics.
- Tests verify scheduling cadence, manual trigger behavior, and output determinism.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:07.576921402Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T08:00:21.224675581Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T08:00:21.224655514Z","close_reason":"Implemented periodic triage loop and on-demand command. TriageLoop and TriageCommand share RemediationPipeline. 12 new tests, 0 clippy warnings.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["maintenance","operations","process_triage","rch-rel-2026q1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:17.006389954Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":231,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claimed by RainyHorse. Starting periodic triage loop and on-demand triage command implementation.","created_at":"2026-02-18T07:44:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.5.5","title":"Feed multi-signal reliability debt into worker health/selection penalties","description":"Incorporate unresolved reliability debt into worker score/eligibility so degraded nodes are not repeatedly selected. Expand from process-only penalties to a unified multi-signal health model that combines process-triage debt, repo-convergence confidence, disk-pressure admission risk, and heartbeat/liveness evidence. Include quarantine/recovery hysteresis so recovered workers re-enter service safely without flapping.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Selection model aggregates multi-signal reliability debt (process, convergence, disk, heartbeat) into deterministic health states and penalties.
- Health states include at least healthy, degraded, quarantined, and probing-recovery with explicit transition rules.
- Penalties and quarantine windows are bounded and decay over time to avoid permanent exclusion from transient incidents.
- If all workers are unsafe/degraded, scheduler preserves fail-open local fallback with explicit reason-coded diagnostics.
- Unit/integration/E2E tests validate mixed-signal failure combinations, flapping resistance, and smooth reintegration after recovery, with structured decision logs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:07.777582653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:05:01.400104Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["health","process_triage","quarantine","rch-rel-2026q1","reliability","selection"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:04:12.973954592Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:04:13.174224679Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:17.205757824Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:17.405356225Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:04:13.373597276Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":179,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Optimization revision: this bead now enforces a unified worker-health controller from multiple reliability signals (not just process debt), reducing repeated failed remote attempts and improving user-perceived stability under partial fleet degradation.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:04:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.5.6","title":"Add tests for detection precision and remediation safety boundaries","description":"Add test coverage for stuck-process detection precision and remediation safety boundaries across unit/integration/E2E levels. Distinguish benign long-running workloads from pathological orphaned/hung process states and verify bounded remediation behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests cover detector confidence scoring and false-positive protections.
- Integration tests cover remediation ladder behavior with auditing and guardrails.
- E2E tests validate triage + scheduler-penalty interactions on degraded workers.
- Test logs include process evidence inputs, classification confidence, chosen action, and post-action worker health.
- Acceptance target is deterministic decisioning with low false positives and no unsafe kill patterns.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:07.983669413Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:48.503837260Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["process_triage","rch-rel-2026q1","safety","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1yt6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:48.503771917Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:07:29.926511645Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:20.435984999Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:20.637966592Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:16.155522943Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:13.951011737Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":192,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Dependency tightened: tests now explicitly require bounded-remediation implementation (bd-vvmd.5.3) so safety assertions validate the real executor behavior, not stubs.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:14Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.5.7","title":"Implement worker build-heartbeat channel for stuck-process precision","description":"Add worker build-heartbeat/liveness signaling so daemon can distinguish slow-progress builds from genuinely stuck processes. Use heartbeat staleness and progress evidence as primary detector inputs before remediation escalation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Worker emits periodic heartbeat/progress metadata during remote execution.
- Daemon records heartbeat freshness and integrates it into stuck-process classification.
- Detector confidence model treats missing heartbeat as one signal among multiple evidence sources.
- Remediation policy requires stronger evidence when heartbeat indicates ongoing progress.
- Unit/integration tests cover heartbeat jitter, temporary drops, prolonged stalls, and false-positive resistance.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:39:45.555508232Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T20:12:54.625848088Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T20:12:54.625829223Z","close_reason":"Completed: heartbeat channel + confidence-based stuck remediation integrated and validated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["daemon","detection","process_triage","rch-rel-2026q1","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.7","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:15.722176963Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":165,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"User-outcome rationale: heartbeat evidence reduces false-positive stuck-process kills and preserves legitimate long-running builds while still enabling safe remediation.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:37Z"}]}
